questions.question: A cylinder has a radius of 5 cm and a height of 12 cm. What is its volume in cubic centimeters? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h. V = 3.14 × 5² × 12 = 3.14 × 25 × 12 = 3.14 × 300 = 942 cm³.

questions.question: A rectangular prism has dimensions 4 m × 6 m × 9 m. If you double each dimension, by what factor does the volume increase?
questions.explanation: Original volume = 4 × 6 × 9 = 216 m³. New volume = 8 × 12 × 18 = 1728 m³. Ratio = 1728 ÷ 216 = 8. Scaling each dimension by 2 scales volume by 2³ = 8.

questions.question: A cone has a base radius of 3 inches and a height of 8 inches. What is its volume in cubic inches? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
A sphere has a diameter of 10 cm. Express its volume in terms of π.
A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 20 cm² and a height (length) of 7 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = base area × height. V = 20 × 7 = 140 cm³. The shape of the base doesn't matter; only its area and the prism's length.

questions.question: A cylinder has a volume of 144π cubic feet and a height of 9 feet. What is its radius in feet?
questions.explanation: Volume = πr²h, so 144π = πr²(9). Divide by π: 144 = 9r². Thus r² = 16, so r = 4 feet.
